steps.title: Set up the cost expression for each item type
steps.content:
Containers cost $1.87 each, so $c$ containers cost $1.87c$ dollars. Tape costs $2.40 per roll, so $t$ rolls cost $2.40t$ dollars. The total amount Leo spends is:
$$1.87c + 2.40t$$

steps.title: Apply the budget constraint
steps.content:
Leo has $15 total to spend, which is his budget limit. The total cost cannot exceed this amount. When a quantity cannot exceed a limit, we use ≤:
$$1.87c + 2.40t \le 15$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
